title: Mentoring with Autonomy
description: Coaching a senior who already knows the answer — listening them into their own decision.
summary_pt_br: Mentoria com autonomia: pergunte, reflita, aguente silêncio, uma observação por sessão, conselho só se pedido.

scenes.subtitle_en: Coaching a senior who already knows the answer — listening them into their own decision.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Mentoria com autonomia: pergunte, reflita, aguente silêncio, uma observação por sessão, conselho só se pedido.

scenes.text_en: Coaching a senior who already knows the answer — listening them into their own decision. Focus: mentoring with autonomy. What do you think the right next step is, honestly?
scenes.text_pt: Mentoria com autonomia: pergunte, reflita, aguente silêncio, uma observação por sessão, conselho só se pedido. Foco: mentoring with autonomy. O que você acha que é o próximo passo, sinceramente?

scenes.title: Mentoring with Autonomy
scenes.body_en: Autonomy-preserving mentoring: ASK, don't tell. REFLECT what you hear. HOLD silence. Offer ONE observation per session. NEVER give advice unless explicitly asked.
scenes.body_pt: Mentoria com autonomia: pergunte, reflita, aguente silêncio, uma observação por sessão, conselho só se pedido.

examples.en: What do you think the right next step is, honestly?
examples.pt: O que você acha que é o próximo passo, sinceramente?

examples.en: I hear you saying the team isn't ready — is that right?
examples.pt: Ouço você dizendo que o time não está pronto — é isso?

examples.en: Would you like my observation, or space to sit with this?
examples.pt: Quer minha observação ou espaço pra sentar com isso?

exercise.question: Complete: "What do you think the right next step is, ___?"
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "What do you think the right next step is, honestly?"
exercise.hint_pt_br: O que você acha que é o próximo passo, sinceramente?

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
I hear you saying the team isn't ready — is that right?
exercise.explanation: Correct: "I hear you saying the team isn't ready — is that right?"
exercise.hint_pt_br: Ouço você dizendo que o time não está pronto — é isso?

exercise.explanation: Key terms from the lesson — match each to its meaning.

scenes.title: Mentoring with Autonomy — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre mentoring with autonomy. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Kumar — your mentee is stuck between two options.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: What do you think the right next step is?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: I hear that both options scare you.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: You want to give advice. Don't.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Would you like my observation, or space?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Autonomy-preserving mentoring is patience with conviction.

scenes.title: The mentoring session
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, análise, e sua opinião crítica. O professor avaliará.
scenes.prompt_en: Describe four autonomy-preserving moves. Use at least three mentoring terms.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Análise crítica com quem falou, argumentos, e sua reflexão.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: On a Tuesday afternoon, Kumar met with a former student who had since become a senior executive at her own firm.

paragraphs.speaker: Mentee
paragraphs.en: I'm stuck between two options and I've been circling this decision for three weeks without making any progress.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: What do you think the right next step is, honestly? I'm genuinely asking — I don't have the answer for you.

paragraphs.speaker: Mentee
paragraphs.en: I think I've already decided, actually — but I've been looking for permission to trust that decision openly.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Would you like my observation, or space to sit with that realization a little longer before moving on?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Autonomy-preserving mentoring, Kumar believed, is the slow discipline of listening someone into the answer they already held.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the advanced class just finished "Mentoring with Autonomy". Coaching a senior who already knows the answer — listening them into their own decision. What do you think the right next step is, honestly? I hear you saying the team isn't ready — is that right? Mr. Kumar reminded the students that at this level, reading widely and writing daily are non-negotiable.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is non-negotiable at this level?
check.explanation: At C-level, wide reading and daily writing are essential.
check.hint_pt_br: Leitura ampla e escrita diária são essenciais no C.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in advanced class we studied "Mentoring with Autonomy". It was demanding but genuinely rewarding. See you next session!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
check.explanation: She says the title: "Mentoring with Autonomy".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala o título: "Mentoring with Autonomy".

scenes.title: Go deeper — advanced resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes avançadas pra aprofundar. Leitura regular é o que separa um aluno avançado de um proficiente.
